sources.special: The Old Man and the Pool

words.definition: Abnormally low muscle tone; dangerously dehydrated or lacking normal tension
words.context: I was hypotonic, which means I was dangerously dehydrated.

words.definition: Officially declared a saint by the Catholic Church; elevated to authoritative status
words.context: And then they get canonized, if you're lucky, and chances are you find out five minutes before you die.

words.definition: Relating to myths or mythology; legendary, larger than life
words.context: He was almost like a mythological creature.

words.definition: A physician specializing in disorders of the nervous system
words.context: And the neurologist came in, and she goes, 'Vince! We're going to do a spinal tap.'

words.definition: Half of a sphere; specifically the two halves of the brain
words.context: He took out a three-dimensional model of the brain, explained the hemispheres of the brain.

words.definition: The ability to float in water; resilience, cheerfulness
words.source: The Old Man and the Pool (2023)
words.context: Then your body sort of floats to the top 'cause the human body has neutral buoyancy.

words.definition: The simultaneous presence of two or more medical conditions in a patient
words.source: The Old Man and the Pool (2023)
words.context: They're both comorbidities, and the thing about comorbidities, sometimes they team up to form a single morbidity.

words.definition: Endoscopic examination of the bladder using a cystoscope inserted through the urethra
words.source: The Old Man and the Pool (2023)
words.context: I'll have you come into the hospital tomorrow morning and give you anesthesia for a cystoscopy.

words.definition: Cancerous; tending to become progressively worse and cause death
words.source: The Old Man and the Pool (2023)
words.context: It was a malignant tumor in my bladder, but I actually was very lucky 'cause they caught it early.

words.definition: Preserved from decay with chemicals, especially for burial
words.source: The Old Man and the Pool (2023)
words.context: I remember being at the church, looking at his body, and it's embalmed.

words.definition: The art of preserving and mounting animal skins for display
words.source: The Old Man and the Pool (2023)
words.context: If we're gonna manipulate the body, why not taxidermy?

words.definition: Having or showing good judgment; wise and careful
words.source: The Old Man and the Pool (2023)
words.context: It is to be judicious with the length of one's breath when holding one's breath.

words.definition: Relating to or affecting the lungs
words.source: The Old Man and the Pool (2023)
words.context: It's a pulmonary test, and there's a ball in the tube, and it simulates blowing out a candle.

words.definition: Abnormal enlargement of veins within the scrotum
words.etymology: Latin: varix (dilated vein) + Greek: kēlē (tumor, swelling)
words.context: You're gonna have to have what's called a varicocele repair.

words.definition: Characterized by belief that parts of something are interconnected and can only be understood as a whole
words.context: We went to this holistic birthing education class, which wasn't a great fit.

words.definition: The relationship between cause and effect; the principle that everything has a cause
words.context: It's that your dad maybe didn't want to be a dad, and he doesn't understand causality that well.

words.definition: The path followed by an object in motion; the course or direction of something
words.context: I feel like she might be one of the people who changes that trajectory.

words.definition: A gathering of people for religious worship; an assembly or collection
words.context: In our house, there is always a congregation of ants summiting around a noodle.

metadata.notes: Birbiglia's vocabulary reflects his conversational storytelling style punctuated by medical terminology (from his health issues), philosophical reflection (on mortality, parenthood, family), and occasional literary/religious references. Words selected demonstrate Latin/Greek etymology, technical precision, and thoughtful introspection characteristic of his comedy.
